segments.caption: The video opens with a sleek black Brabus 900 sedan on display at an auto show, its polished surface reflecting the bright lights, with a large 'BRABUS' sign and the number '900' projected on the floor. The scene transitions to an aerial view of a Brabus facility, showcasing rows of luxury vehicles parked outside a modern building with a prominent 'BRABUS' sign. Inside the workshop, a white Brabus G-Wagen is shown with its hood open, revealing a powerful engine, while a technician works nearby. The focus shifts to a black Brabus G-Wagen being worked on, with a mechanic adjusting components under the hood. The video then shows a black Brabus sedan on a dynamometer, undergoing performance testing, with a technician monitoring the process. A man in a Brabus-branded vest speaks to the camera, gesturing as he explains the process, with several Brabus vehicles in the background. The scene cuts to a close-up of a mechanic installing a large yellow brake caliper onto a wheel, followed by another mechanic using a power tool to work on a car's interior. The video continues with a technician using a 3D scanner on a car part, then a close-up of a hand sanding a white car part in a spray booth. The final scenes show a mechanic working on an engine with a tattooed arm, and a wide shot of a high-tech workshop with CNC machines and various tools, emphasizing precision engineering. The video concludes with a close-up of a hand using a tool to adjust a component on a car's interior, highlighting the meticulous craftsmanship involved in Brabus modifications.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a glossy black car wheel, reflecting the workshop environment, before transitioning to a wide shot of a warehouse-like space filled with stacked tires and boxes. A technician wearing a black Brabus-branded shirt with 'BRABUS' printed in white on the back walks through the area, retrieving a large cardboard box and then a silver multi-spoke alloy wheel. He carries the wheel to a tire mounting machine, where he carefully places it on the machine’s hub. He then retrieves a new black tire, rolls it to the machine, and mounts it onto the wheel using the machine’s mechanical arms. The process is shown in detail, with close-ups of the tire being pressed onto the rim and the machine’s clamps securing it. The technician then inflates the tire, checks its pressure, and ensures it is properly seated. Throughout the process, the workshop environment is visible, with shelves of parts, safety signage, and industrial equipment. The technician, identified by on-screen text as Markus Oczyllok, a Tyre Mechanic, speaks directly to the camera, explaining the process with a focused and professional demeanor. The lighting is bright and functional, typical of a high-precision automotive workshop, emphasizing the clean, organized, and technical nature of the work being performed.

segments.caption: The video segment opens with Thomas Schreiber, a motor mechanic in a black Brabus-branded polo shirt with orange 'BRABUS' logos and blue nitrile gloves, operating the Sunnen CK-21 Brabus Cylinder Honing Machine. He carefully secures a large aluminum engine block with hex bolts, then lowers the honing tool into one of the cylinder bores while coolant sprays to reduce friction. The machine’s flexible black hose extracts debris as the tool rotates, smoothing the bore’s interior. A close-up reveals the engine block’s clean, honed cylinder bores after processing. The scene transitions to a different area of the workshop where Sven Bruckschlegel, Head of Engine Design, wearing a black Brabus polo shirt, inspects the engine block using a borescope connected to a Sony monitor branded with 'BRABUS'. segments.caption: The video segment opens with a close-up view from beneath a vehicle elevated on a hydraulic lift, where Klaus Pachall, identified as a Quality Inspector, is meticulously inspecting the undercarriage. He wears a black Brabus-branded coat over a white shirt and uses a flashlight to examine the suspension, exhaust, and drivetrain components. The camera follows his movements as he points to specific parts, including the wheel hub and brake assembly, emphasizing precision and attention to detail. He then moves to the side of the vehicle, a black Brabus G-Wagen, and uses a tablet to check data or reference specifications while inspecting the wheel and tire. The scene transitions to him lowering the lift using the control panel, then proceeding to clean the vehicle’s exterior, including the rear exhaust tips and alloy wheels, with a microfiber cloth. The environment is a bright, clean workshop with large windows, industrial equipment, and a visible blue trash bin labeled 'SCHREINER'. The lighting is even and professional, highlighting the vehicle’s glossy black paint and intricate details. On-screen text identifies Klaus Pachall and his role, reinforcing the quality control process. The segment concludes with him wiping the wheel, showcasing the final touches before the vehicle’s release.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a man in a black Brabus-branded coat, identified as Klaus Pachall, the Quality Inspector, meticulously wiping the glossy black surface of a Brabus G-Wagen with a blue microfiber cloth. The setting is a bright, clean workshop with white walls and industrial lighting, suggesting a high-end automotive facility. He moves to the rear of the vehicle, cleaning the exhaust tips, then walks toward the driver’s side, where he opens the door and begins inspecting the interior. The interior is strikingly luxurious, featuring vibrant blue leather seats with quilted stitching and a matching blue dashboard. Klaus carefully examines the door panel, seat, and center console, using a flashlight to inspect details. He then opens the hood, revealing the engine bay, and continues his inspection with a tablet in hand, likely cross-referencing specifications. The camera captures his focused expression and precise movements, emphasizing the thoroughness of the quality control process. Signs on the wall, including 'WASCHHALLE', 'SATTLEREI', and 'SCHREINEREI', indicate the various departments within the Brabus facility. The overall atmosphere is one of precision, cleanliness, and attention to detail, highlighting the final stages of vehicle preparation before delivery.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a technician in a black Brabus-branded shirt adjusting the fiber-optic ceiling of a Brabus G-Wagen, which is positioned on a hydraulic lift inside a clean, well-lit workshop. The camera then pans to show the vehicle’s front, highlighting its aggressive black grille with a central 'B' emblem, circular LED headlights, and a 'BRABUS 246' test number on the windshield. The scene transitions to an outdoor test track, marked by a red and white sign reading 'BRABUS TESTSTRECKE Zutritt verboten! TEST TRACK No trespassing!', as the Brabus G-Wagen drives onto the paved area. The vehicle maneuvers through a series of marked turns and cones, showcasing its handling. Interior shots reveal a driver in a black Brabus-branded jacket gripping a white-wrapped steering wheel, with a vibrant blue leather interior featuring quilted stitching and a matching dashboard. The G-Wagen continues its test drive, navigating a cobblestone path and a sloped road surrounded by greenery, demonstrating its off-road capability. The camera captures dynamic side and rear views as the vehicle accelerates and turns, emphasizing its powerful performance and sleek design. The segment concludes with the G-Wagen driving away on a paved road, framed by trees and a yellow curb, under overcast skies.

segments.caption: The video opens with Kevin Kalinowski, a car maintenance technician, standing in a tiled wash bay, gesturing toward a metal rack holding large white SOHAX containers filled with colored cleaning solutions—blue, pink, and yellow—each equipped with orange spigots. He is wearing a black Brabus-branded long-sleeve shirt and dark pants. The scene transitions to him using a high-pressure washer to rinse a black Brabus G-Wagen, which is parked inside the wash hall. Water sprays forcefully, creating a misty effect as he cleans the vehicle’s rear and side. He then switches to a large green sponge, scrubbing the soapy surface of the G-Wagen’s rear and front, focusing on the grille and hood. After scrubbing, he uses a rotary polisher with a white foam pad to buff the car’s glossy black paint, moving methodically over the hood and front fender. The vehicle’s surface becomes increasingly reflective. The final shot shows the gleaming Brabus G-Wagen being driven out of the wash bay and onto a white transport trailer, with the driver visible inside. The trailer is part of a larger white transport truck, and the vehicle is carefully positioned on the ramp before the trailer door closes. The environment is clean and industrial, with white tiled walls, a wet floor, and natural light entering from the open bay door, revealing a parking lot with other vehicles outside.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a man in a black Brabus-branded puffer vest with red 'BRABUS' logos on the sleeves and back, wearing white gloves, securing a spare tire onto the rear of a black Brabus G-Wagen using a blue ratchet strap. He carefully tightens the strap and then walks away as the ramp of a white transport trailer is lowered. The scene transitions to a wide shot of a black Brabus-branded Mercedes-Benz truck, prominently displaying 'BRABUS HIGH PERFORMANCE AUTOMOBILES' and the website 'www.brabus.com' on its side and rear. The truck, with its sleek black cab and matching trailer, drives forward on a paved lot, passing a small orange traffic cone and a gated entrance. The camera follows the truck as it turns and exits the facility, revealing a modern industrial complex with a white roof and gray walls, surrounded by trees and parked vehicles. On-screen text appears on the left side, identifying the video as 'The High-End Tuners from the Ruhr Valley,' listing production credits for True Jokx Productions, Johannes Kudernatsch, Thomas Riek, editor Olmo Heinecke, and direction by Heiko Sedlack (WELT), with the WELT.DE logo at the bottom. The truck continues to drive away, emphasizing the professional and high-end nature of the Brabus operation.
